subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a design solution that reduces the impacts of a weather-related hazard.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world.
description_plain: Your child can gather and combine information to describe what the weather is like in different parts of the world.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season.
description_plain: Your child can show weather data in tables and graphs to explain what weather is usually like in a season.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes and the types of plants and animals that live there may change.
description_plain: Your child can explain if a solution is good for helping plants and animals when their environment changes.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence how some organisms thrive, some struggle to survive, and some cannot survive in a particular habitat.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence why some animals do well, some have a hard time, and some cannot live in a certain place.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence and reasoning to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving, finding mates, and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how differences in traits among animals of the same kind help them survive, find mates, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of the organisms and the environments in which they lived long ago.
description_plain: Your child can study fossils to learn about animals and environments from long ago.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence and reasoning to support the explanation that traits can be influenced by the environment.
description_plain: Your child understands that traits can be affected by the environment and can explain this using evidence.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence that plants and animals have traits inherited from parents and that variations of these traits exist in a group of similar organisms.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to show that plants and animals get traits from their parents and that these traits can vary.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that some animals form groups that help members survive.
description_plain: Your child can explain why some animals live in groups to help each other survive.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles but all have in common birth, growth, reproduction, and death.
description_plain: Your child can create models to show that all living things have life stages like birth, growth, reproduction, and death.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem that can be solved by applying scientific ideas about magnets.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that can be solved using ideas about magnets.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions about cause and effect relationships of electric or magnetic interactions between two objects not in contact with each other.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions about how electric or magnetic forces can cause effects between two objects that are not touching.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements of an object's motion to provide evidence for how a pattern can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child can watch and measure how an object moves and use patterns to predict how it will move next.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out an investigation to provide evidence of the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do an experiment to show how balanced and unbalanced forces affect how an object moves.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions to reduce the impacts of natural Earth processes on humans.
description_plain: Your child comes up with and compares different ideas to lessen how natural Earth events impact people.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe that energy and fuels are derived from natural resources and their uses affect the environment.
description_plain: Your child will gather information to explain that energy and fuels come from natural resources and that using them can affect the environment.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from maps to describe patterns of Earth's features.
description_plain: Your child looks at maps and data to describe patterns in Earth’s features.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements to provide evidence of the effects of weathering or the rate of erosion by water, ice, wind, or vegetation.
description_plain: Your child observes or measures how weathering or erosion by water, ice, wind, or plants changes the land.

subject: Science
description: Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations and fossils in rock layers to support an explanation for changes in a landscape over time.
description_plain: Your child uses patterns in rocks and fossils to explain how a landscape has changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to describe that animals receive different types of information through their senses, process the information in their brain, and respond to the information in different ways.
description_plain: Your child uses a model to show how animals get information through their senses, process it in their brain, and respond in different ways.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that plants and animals have internal and external structures that function to support survival, growth, behavior, and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child explains how plants and animals have body parts inside and outside that help them survive, grow, behave, and reproduce.

subject: Science
description: Create and compare multiple solutions that use patterns to transfer information.
description_plain: Your child can create and compare different ways to send information using patterns.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe how light reflecting from objects and entering the eye allows objects to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can make a model to show how light bounces off things and enters the eye so we can see objects.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model of waves to describe patterns in terms of amplitude and wavelength and to provide evidence that waves can cause objects to move.
description_plain: Your child can make a model of waves to show their height and length and explain how waves can make things move.

subject: Science
description: Design, test, and refine a device that converts energy from one form to another.
description_plain: Your child can create and improve a simple tool that changes energy from one type to another.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and predict outcomes about the changes in energy that occur when objects collide.
description_plain: Your child will ask questions and guess what will happen to energy when objects bump into each other.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to provide evidence for how energy can be transferred from place to place by sound, light, heat, and electric currents.
description_plain: Your child will observe and explain how energy moves through sound, light, heat, and electricity.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation relating the speed of an object to the energy of that object.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence to explain how the speed of something relates to its energy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use vocabulary acquired through conversations, reading, and learning experiences, to ask questions and convey ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses new words learned from talking, reading, and experiences to ask questions and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child acts out different action words that mean similar things, like walk, march, or prance, to understand their differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use
description_plain: Your child recognizes how words connect to real-life situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child shows they understand common action and describing words by knowing their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups, like putting all the animals together or all the foods together.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, explore word relationships and subtle differences in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child explores how words relate to each other and notices small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child uses common word endings and beginnings, like -ed or un-, to help figure out new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).
description_plain: Your child learns new meanings for familiar words and uses them correctly, like knowing 'duck' is a bird and also a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what new or tricky words mean by using clues from what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2
description_plain: Begins in grade 2

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child spells simple words by sounding them out and matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child writes letters that match most consonant and short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks that come at the end of sentences, like periods and question marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: With help, your child capitalizes the first word in a sentence and the word 'I'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can say and help make complete sentences when talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions
description_plain: Your child uses common small words that show location or time, like 'in' or 'on'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives)
description_plain: Your child understands and uses question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/
description_plain: Your child says plural nouns by adding sounds like /s/ or /es/ to words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use common naming words (nouns) and action words (verbs).

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all uppercase (capital) and lowercase letters of the alphabet using correct formation.
description_plain: Your child prints all uppercase and lowercase letters correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly and share their thoughts, feelings, and ideas so others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events. With prompting and support, provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child describes familiar people, places, things, and events, and with help, adds more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to get help, find out information, or clear up confusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding from a source of information by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand information and asks for help if something is unclear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by taking turns and responding to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child can follow simple rules when talking with others during group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child will join in conversations with classmates and adults about kindergarten topics and stories, both in small and larger groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 1
description_plain: Begins in grade 1

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4
description_plain: Begins in grade 4

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support provide a response to a question using a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing by recalling information from experiences or gathering information from provided sources.
description_plain: With help, your child can answer questions by drawing, telling, or writing about things they remember or learn from given information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: Your child will take part in group research and writing activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, individually and collaboratively explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing.
description_plain: With support, your child can use different digital tools alone or with others to create and share their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing.
description_plain: With help, your child listens to friends’ ideas and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3
description_plain: Begins in grade 3

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, talking, and writing to tell about something that happened, put the events in order, and share how they felt about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, talking, and writing to share facts about a topic and name what they are writing about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, talking, and writing to share their opinion about a topic or book and tell the reader what it is about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the story to check and fix words they read, reading again if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and prosody (stress, phrasing, intonation and expression) on consecutive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read kindergarten-level books out loud with correct words, speed, and expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads kindergarten-level books with understanding and a clear purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read simple beginner books accurately and smoothly to understand the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level appropriate high-frequency words by sight.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common kindergarten words by sight.

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds for the five vowels.
description_plain: Your child knows the long and short sounds for the five vowels (a, e, i, o, u).

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary sound for each consonant.
description_plain: Your child can say the main sound for each consonant letter.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about letters and sounds to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme words (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) to make new words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x.
description_plain: Your child can change or add sounds in simple three-letter words to make new words, except those ending with l, r, or x.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in simple three-letter words, except those ending with l, r, or x.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together the beginning and ending sounds of one-syllable spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, say, put together, and break apart the parts (syllables) in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme, like 'cat' and 'hat.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands that words have parts like syllables and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can point out the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet. e. Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can name all uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet and identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that there are spaces between words when we write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child knows that spoken words are written down using letters in a certain order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works, like how to follow words on a page and what letters and words look like.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: With some help, your child can choose books to read for fun, interest, or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficultly and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literary features, knowledge demands.)
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level informational texts with help, even when the text gets harder or more complex.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year read and comprehends a variety of informational text.
description_plain: Your child will read and understand different kinds of informational books by the end of the year.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support compare and contrast two texts on the same topic (similarities and differences).
description_plain: Your child can compare two texts about the same topic and talk about how they are alike and different with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the details/evidence an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find and talk about details the author uses to support ideas in a text with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures and words work together in an informational book with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author and illustrator and define the role of each in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who wrote and who drew the pictures in an informational book and what each person did.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, know and use various text features.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use different parts of a book, like headings or pictures, with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don't know in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main idea of a book and remember important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose books to read for fun, interest, or schoolwork with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficultly and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literary features, knowledge demands.)
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level stories and poems with help, even when they get harder or more complex.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year read and comprehend a variety of literary text.
description_plain: Your child will read and understand different kinds of stories and poems by the end of the year.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, explain the relationship between illustrations and the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures and words work together in a story or poem with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who wrote and who drew the pictures in a story and what each person did.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, explain differences between common types of texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different kinds of stories and texts are not the same with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in stories, poems, nursery rhymes, or songs.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about new words in stories, poems, or songs with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe characters, settings, and major events in a story.
description_plain: Your child can describe the characters, places, and main events in a story with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: With help, your child can retell familiar stories and include important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases, including frequently occurring conjunctions to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use words and joining words like 'and' or 'but' to share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish r differences among verbs and adjectives with similar meanings (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl); by defining, choosing, or acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action and describing words by defining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use.
description_plain: Your child can show how words connect to real-life situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes
description_plain: Your child can explain what a word means by saying what group it belongs to and its important features.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups to better understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and subtle differences in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand how words relate to each other and notice small differences in their meanings with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common root words and their different forms, like 'look,' 'looks,' 'looked,' and 'looking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common word parts (like 'un-' or '-ed') to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the rest of the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose a strategy to determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade level content.
description_plain: Your child will use different ways to figure out the meaning of new or tricky words they find in their reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2
description_plain: Begins in grade 2

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns. e. Spell grade-appropriate high-frequency words f. Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly, spell frequently used words for their grade, and sound out new words to spell them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates
description_plain: Use commas in dates

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use the correct punctuation at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence, the pronoun I, dates, and names of people.
description_plain: Your child knows to use capital letters at the start of sentences, for the word 'I,' for dates, and for people's names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand different types of sentences (statements, questions, commands, and exclamations) when asked.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child uses common small words that show location or time, like 'during,' 'beyond,' and 'toward.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (e.g., articles - a, an, the; demonstratives - this, that, these, those).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'a,' 'an,' 'the,' 'this,' 'that,' 'these,' and 'those' to point out or talk about things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child uses common connecting words like 'and,' 'but,' 'or,' 'so,' and 'because' to join ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words, like 'big' or 'happy,' in their sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child uses verbs to talk about actions that happened in the past, are happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their; anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'I,' 'me,' 'my,' 'they,' 'them,' 'their,' and words like 'anyone' and 'everything' to talk about people and things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child uses singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences, like 'He hops' and 'We hop.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use regular nouns, names of people or places, and show when something belongs to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all uppercase (capital) and lowercase letters correctly and fluently. Space letters, words, and sentences appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can write all capital and small letters correctly and neatly, leaving proper spaces between letters, words, and sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses basic grammar rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 1 Language standard 1 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can say full sentences when it fits the situation or task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create drawings or other visual displays when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas, feelings, or thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child can clearly describe people, places, things, and events with helpful details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone else says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud, information presented orally, or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in stories or information they hear or see.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions if they are confused about what is being discussed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations by responding to what others say, going back and forth several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child follows agreed rules when talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations about grade level topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child joins in group talks about topics and stories with friends and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, write routinely to increase stamina.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, both individually and collaboratively.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.

description_plain: Your child gives a reason to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and prosody (stress, phrasing, intonation, and expression) on consecutive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books out loud accurately and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read first grade books with understanding and for a reason.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate high frequency words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words they see often.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have endings like -ed, -ing, or -s.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can sound out two-syllable words by breaking them into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and can count the syllables in a word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows that a silent 'e' or vowel pairs can make vowel sounds long.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read simple one-syllable words that follow regular spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant blends and digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to sound out common consonant blends and pairs of letters that make one sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use first grade phonics skills to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes). e. Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can break a one-syllable word into all its sounds and change sounds to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the first, middle, and last sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say whole one-syllable words by putting together individual sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in words with only one syllable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made of sounds and syllables when listening and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that sentences are made of words put together in a meaningful sequence. b. Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child understands that sentences are made of words in a meaningful order and can identify the first word, capital letters, and ending punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized, like reading left to right and knowing what a word or sentence looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: With help, your child can pick books they like for fun or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literacy features, and knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child reads and understands grade-level texts that get a little harder over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a variety of informational text.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child reads and understands different kinds of informational books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find ways two texts about the same topic are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the details/evidence an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can point out details the author uses to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a book to talk about the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the difference between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how pictures and words give different information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use things like headings, tables of contents, or bold words to find important information in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to figure out what words and phrases mean in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell you the main idea of a text and share important details from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose books to read on their own for fun, interest, or schoolwork with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literacy features, and knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level books that get a little harder over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a variety of literary text.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand different kinds of stories and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a story to describe the characters, where it happens, or what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details to identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details to tell who is telling the story at different parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between common types of texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain big differences between common types of books or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories, poems, or songs that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words or phrases in stories, poems, or songs that show feelings or describe senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe the characters, where the story happens, and the main events using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including important details, and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use acquired words and phrases, including adjectives and adverbs to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use new words, including describing words and action words, to share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish subtle differences among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child can tell the small differences between similar action words and describing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life examples, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and subtle differences in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use print and digital reference materials to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find or check the meaning of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child can guess the meaning of compound words by knowing the meanings of the smaller words inside them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child can use a word they know to help figure out the meaning of a new word with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words when a prefix is added (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child can understand how adding a prefix changes the meaning of a word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the rest of the sentence to help figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade level content using flexible strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening, including making comparisons between formal and informal English.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use language correctly when reading, writing, speaking, or listening, including when to use formal or informal words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult print and digital reference materials, as needed to check and correct spellings
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage - badge; boy - boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned when writing new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade appropriate high frequency words correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell common second-grade words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and singular possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to make contractions and show ownership for one person or thing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters. Use commas to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas in letter greetings and closings, and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of holidays, products, and places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child can write and change simple and compound sentences in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words (adjectives) and words that tell how something is done (adverbs), choosing the right one.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child can use the past tense of common irregular verbs like 'sat,' 'hid,' and 'told.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child can use reflexive pronouns like 'myself' and 'ourselves' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child can use irregular plural nouns like 'feet,' 'children,' 'teeth,' 'mice,' and 'fish.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group, herd, class).
description_plain: Your child can use words that name groups of things, like 'group,' 'herd,' or 'class.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Print letters correctly and fluently. Space words and sentences properly so that writing can be read easily by others.
description_plain: Your child can write letters neatly and space words and sentences so others can read their writing easily.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 2 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences and add details or explanations when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings, visual displays, performances, or media presentations of stories or poems to express ideas, thoughts, and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with relevant facts and descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer appropriate questions in response to a speaker in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
