description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or correct words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and prosody (stress, phrasing, intonation, and expression) on consecutive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text out loud smoothly and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and a clear reason for reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate high frequency words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads common words that appear often in grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot and read words where the spelling and sounds don’t always match but are common.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can sound out words that have common beginnings or endings like prefixes and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels and short vowels. .
description_plain: Your child can sound out two-syllable words with long and short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read words with common pairs of vowels (like 'ea' or 'ai').

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills for their grade to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 2 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: There is no grade 2 standard for this; please check earlier grades for this skill.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 2 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: There is no grade 2 standard for this skill; please refer to earlier grades.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select text for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: With help, your child can choose books or texts they like for fun, interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language features, and knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child reads and understands grade-level texts that get harder and more complex over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a variety of informational text.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child reads and understands different kinds of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the main ideas presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences between the main ideas in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how details/evidence support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how details in a text help support the author's main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., photographs, charts, diagrams contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how pictures, charts, or diagrams help explain and make the text clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and what they want to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use things like headings, bold words, and tables of contents to find important information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases related to grade 2 topics when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps are connected in a nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea of a nonfiction text and what each paragraph is mostly about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select text for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose books to read on their own for fun, interest, or school work with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literary features, knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child reads and understands grade-level books that get a little harder over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a variety of literary text.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child will read and understand different kinds of stories and books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in books or on screens to show they understand the characters, setting, or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Express differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud. Describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child can tell how characters see things differently and can read their dialogue using different voices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the overall structure of stories and their common elements (e.g., how the beginning introduces the story, how the action leads to the resolution).
description_plain: Your child can identify the parts of a story, like how it starts, what happens, and how it ends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases create rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases create rhythm and meaning in stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to big events and problems in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, those from diverse cultures, and determine their central message/theme, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to understand key details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for conversations, school subjects, and special topics, including words that show where or when something happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish subtle differences among closely related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child can tell the small differences between similar words that show feelings or how sure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like describing friendly or helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context.
description_plain: Your child can tell when words or phrases mean exactly what they say or something different (like idioms) in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and subtle differences in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use print and digital reference materials to determine or clarify meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses books or online tools to find out what words and phrases mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child uses the main part of a word to help figure out the meaning of new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words when affixes are added.
description_plain: Your child learns what words mean when prefixes or suffixes are added.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use flexible strategies to determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to understand new or tricky words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between formal and informal English.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between formal and informal English and uses them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that make their writing or speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use high frequency words and spelling patterns to spell grade appropriate words correctly, consulting reference materials as needed.
description_plain: Your child spells common grade-level words correctly and uses resources to help when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Form and use possessives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Use commas in addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use adverbs and adjectives (comparative and superlative).
description_plain: Your child can use describing words and words that tell how something is done, including comparing things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure subjects and verbs match and uses pronouns correctly with the words they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple) verb tenses (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk)
description_plain: Your child uses past, present, and future verb forms correctly, like 'walked,' 'walk,' and 'will walk.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses both regular verbs (like 'walk') and irregular verbs (like 'go').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child uses nouns that name ideas or feelings, like 'childhood.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses plural nouns correctly, including those that change in unusual ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly in print or cursive, using appropriate spacing and margins.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly by hand, leaving proper spaces and margins.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 3 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child will speak in complete sentences when needed to give clear answers or more information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child can record stories or poems reading smoothly and clearly, and add pictures or visuals to highlight important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with relevant facts and, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can talk about a topic or story clearly, sharing important facts and details at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what a speaker says, giving good explanations and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text presented in diverse media and formats, such as visual, quantitative, and oral formats.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details from different types of information, like pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate and problem solve as appropriate for productive group discussions
description_plain: Your child works well with others and helps solve problems during group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and what they understand during a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented. stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to understand better, stays focused, and connects their ideas to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child can follow the agreed rules for group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses what they know to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) on grade level topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, listen to others, build on their ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences; independently select writing topics and formats for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods, choosing topics and styles for fun, school, or other purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4
description_plain: Begins in grade 4

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences and gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences and finds facts from books or the internet, taking short notes and organizing them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child uses computers or tablets to write and share their work and to work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, and editing. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 3.)
description_plain: With help from others, your child can plan, write, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: With support, your child writes clearly and organizes ideas well for the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure conclusion (when appropriate to the genre).
description_plain: Your child can finish a story or writing with a clear ending when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'finally' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions of actions and feelings to make stories and events interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can set up a story by introducing the situation and characters, and organize the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives (e.g. story, poetry, drama) to develop real and imagined experiences or events using descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories or poems that tell about real or imagined events with clear details and order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion.
description_plain: Provide a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade level appropriate linking words and phrase to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect ideas that belong together when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by giving facts, definitions, and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include text features when useful to support comprehension for the reader.
description_plain: Your child will introduce a topic and group related information together, using things like headings or pictures to help the reader understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or give information about a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion.
description_plain: Provide a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use grade level appropriate linking words and phrases to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect their opinion with reasons when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic or book, state their opinion, and organize their reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic or book and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or double-check the meaning and pronunciation of words, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level texts in a variety of genres with accuracy, appropriate rate, and prosody (stress, phrasing, intonation, and expression) on consecutive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books accurately and with expression, paying attention to how the words sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and a clear reason for reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate high frequency words.
description_plain: Your child can read common words that appear often in grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multi-syllable words.
description_plain: Decode multi-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize common beginnings and endings of words and knows what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills to figure out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 3 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: There is no grade 3 standard for this; please check earlier grades for related skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 3 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: There is no grade 3 standard for this; please check earlier grades for related skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select text for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: With some help, your child can choose books to read for fun, interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language features, and knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level nonfiction texts that get harder and more detailed over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend grade level informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level nonfiction books about history, science, and technical topics on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the main ideas and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences in the main ideas and important details of two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text to support particular points the author makes.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs in a text connect to support the author's points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from specific images (e.g., illustrations, maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, maps, or photos along with the words in a text to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author's point of view based on text evidence and distinguish the student's point of view from that of the author.
description_plain: Your child can tell the author's point of view using clues from the text and explain how their own view is different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use things like headings, tables of contents, or search tools to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words and phrases in a text about a third-grade topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationship between events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical procedures text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events or ideas in history, science, or technical texts are connected by time order or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; identify the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text and explain how the important details support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what they read, using the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: With some help, your child can pick stories to read for fun, interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literary features, knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level stories that get harder and more detailed over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend a variety of literary texts.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand many different kinds of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the central message/themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast the main messages, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the pictures in a story help show what is happening or how the characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student's point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters. Identify the point of view in a text and distinguish the student's perspective from that of the narrator or characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story and explain how their own thoughts are different from the narrator or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text using appropriate terminology such as chapter, scene, and stanza and describe how each successive part relates to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, and poems using words like chapter, scene, and stanza, and explain how these parts fit together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when the author uses nonliteral language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story and explain how their actions contribute to the plot.
description_plain: Your child can describe the characters in a story and explain how what they do helps move the story along.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including those from diverse cultures; determine the central message/theme, lesson, or moral and explain how it is revealed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the main message or lesson by pointing out important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what they read in stories, using the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child can correctly use grade-level words, including words that describe actions, feelings, or are important for a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands words better by learning their opposites and words that mean almost the same thing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors (e.g., as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain simple comparisons like similes and metaphors in stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find out how to say words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin parts of words to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 level content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that different situations call for formal (e.g., presenting ideas) or informal (e.g., small-group discussion) English.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like when giving a presentation, and informal English, like when talking with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose punctuation marks to create a certain effect in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that clearly show their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly for their grade level and use references to check if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma before words like 'and,' 'but,' or 'or' when joining two sentences together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing what someone says or quoting from a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.
description_plain: Use correct capitalization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their).
description_plain: Your child uses words that sound alike but have different meanings correctly, like to, too, and two.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child writes complete sentences and fixes mistakes like sentence fragments or run-on sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can make and use phrases that start with prepositions, like 'under the table' or 'after the game.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).
description_plain: Your child puts adjectives in the right order in sentences, like saying 'a small red bag' instead of 'a red small bag.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries auxiliary verbs such as can, may, and must) to convey various conditions to clarify meaning.
description_plain: Your child uses helping verbs like can, may, and must to show different meanings clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive (e.g., I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses verb forms that show ongoing actions, like 'I am walking' or 'I was walking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).
description_plain: Your child uses words like who, which, where, and when to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly and fluently in print or cursive using appropriate spacing and margins.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly and smoothly in print or cursive with proper spacing and margins.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that different situations call for formal (e.g., presenting ideas) or informal (e.g., small-group discussion) English, and use formal English when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 4 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like in presentations, and uses it correctly when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add multimedia components (e.g., audio recordings) and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child adds things like pictures or audio to presentations to help explain main ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can clearly and in order talk about a topic, story, or experience, using facts and details to support their main points and speaking at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can pick out the reasons and evidence a speaker gives to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text presented in diverse media and formats, such as visual, quantitative, and oral formats.
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of a story or information they hear, see, or read in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate and problem solve as appropriate for productive group discussions.
description_plain: Your child works well with others and helps solve problems during group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can review the main ideas from a discussion and explain their own thoughts based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions to better understand the topic, and add comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the group’s rules during discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or learned about the topic, and uses what they know to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) on grade 4 level topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences; independently select writing topics and formats for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, both for short and long projects, choosing topics and styles they enjoy or need for school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 reading standards for informational texts to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading facts and information to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 reading standards for literature to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading stories and literature to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their writing and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences and gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child remembers important details from their experiences and finds useful information from books and the internet, taking notes and listing where they found it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate sufficient keyboarding skills to type.
description_plain: Your child can type well enough to write their work on a computer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interact and collaborate with others
description_plain: Your child works and shares ideas with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and publish writing
description_plain: Produce and publish writing

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single sitting. With guidance and support, use technology, including the internet, to enhance writing.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses computers and the internet to write, share their work, and work with others, typing at least one page at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, and editing. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 4.)
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, writes, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events (when appropriate to the genre).
description_plain: Your child writes endings that make sense based on the story or events they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use specific words and details that help readers picture what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'next,' 'then,' or 'finally' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses conversations and descriptions to show what happens and how characters feel in their stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can set up a story by introducing the situation and characters and organizing the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives (e.g., story, poetry, drama) to develop real and imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories, poems, or plays that tell about real or made-up events with clear details and order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes endings that match the information or explanations they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and vocabulary related to the topic to explain it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas with in paragraphs and sections of information using grade level appropriate words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child connects ideas in their writing using words and phrases that fit their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by adding facts, definitions, examples, or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include text features that support comprehension for the reader.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic and organize related information into paragraphs with helpful features like headings or pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic and share ideas and information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes endings that relate to the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using grade level appropriate words and phrases
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words and phrases that fit their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons for their opinion and support them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, share their opinion, and organize related ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic and give reasons and facts to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the sentence to help figure out or correct words they don't know, and will reread if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level texts in a variety of genres orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and prosody (stress, phrasing, intonation, and expression) on consecutive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books out loud with accuracy and expression, sounding natural and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and for a clear reason.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and correctly so they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words. Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to accurately read unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about sounds and word parts to read new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 4 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: There is no grade 4 standard for this; please see earlier grades for this skill.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 4 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: There is no grade 4 standard for this; please see earlier grades for this skill.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select text for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose books or texts on their own for fun, interest, or school work with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literary features, knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level informational texts that get harder and more detailed over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year read and comprehend a variety of informational texts.
description_plain: Your child will read and understand different kinds of informational texts by the end of the year.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write, speak, or demonstrate knowledge about the subject.
description_plain: Your child can use information from two texts about the same topic to write, talk, or show what they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support their points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in pictures, charts, graphs, or timelines and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the similarities/differences in the point of view based on the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare two accounts of the same event—one from someone who was there and one from someone who wasn’t—and explain how their points of view are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall text structure of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text (description, chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution).
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by order of events, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words or phrases in a text about a fourth grade topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, steps in procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what happened and why in history, science, or technical texts by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain what a text says explicitly and draw inferences by referring to details and examples in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a text clearly says and make guesses based on details and examples in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select text for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can pick stories or books on their own for fun, interest, or school with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g., layout, text structure, language/literary features, knowledge demands).
