description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level stories and poems that get harder and more detailed over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year read and comprehend a variety of literary texts.
description_plain: Your child will read and understand different kinds of stories and poems by the end of the year.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the author's approach to similar themes, topics, and patterns of events in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different authors write about similar ideas or events in stories, myths, or traditional tales from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the print version of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the same text; support with details and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play they read with a movie, play, or audio version and explain the connection using details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different stories are told, including the difference between stories told by a character (first person) and stories told by a narrator (third person).

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between stories, dramas, and poems, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, stanza, rhythm, meter) and dramas (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between stories, plays, and poems, and talk about parts of poems and plays like verses, characters, and dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when they are used in a special way like metaphors or similes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail, using specific information from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem and explain how it is supported by details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem and explain it using details from the text, and also summarize the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain what a text says explicitly and draw inferences by referring to details and examples in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a story or poem clearly says and make guesses based on details and examples in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships (e.g., however, although, nevertheless, similarly, moreover, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can correctly use grade-level words, including words that show contrast, addition, or other relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses the relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what similes and metaphors mean in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language, how words relate to each other, and small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find how to say words correctly and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade level content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain differences in how English is spoken or written in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can change sentences by expanding, combining, or shortening them to make writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly and use references to check spelling if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child can use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to show the titles of books and other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no, to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (e.g., It's true, isn't it?), and to indicate direct address (e.g., Is that you, Steve?).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly when writing yes or no, asking a question at the end of a sentence, or speaking directly to someone in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma after the first part of a sentence when it introduces what comes next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child will use commas correctly to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions (e.g., either/or, neither/nor).
description_plain: Your child uses pairs of connecting words like either/or and neither/nor correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when the verb tense changes incorrectly in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use different verb tenses to show when things happen or their order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect verb tenses (e.g., I had walked; I have walked; I will have walked.
description_plain: Your child can use perfect verb tenses like 'had walked,' 'have walked,' and 'will have walked' in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections in general and use in sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do and can use them in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 5 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, sounds, or other media to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly and at a pace that others can easily understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using appropriate facts and relevant descriptive details to support main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child uses facts and details that relate to the main idea when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sequencing ideas logically.
description_plain: Sequencing ideas logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion.
description_plain: Your child can talk about a topic, share information, or give their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can summarize what a speaker says and explain how their points are supported by reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize text or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information they see, hear, or read in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate and problem solve as appropriate for productive group discussions.
description_plain: Your child works well with others and helps solve problems during group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child can review the main ideas from a discussion and draw conclusions based on what was learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions in a discussion and add helpful comments to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the rules for group discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or studied the topic, and uses what they know to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) on grade level topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences; independently select writing topics and formats for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child will write often, sometimes working on longer projects over several days and sometimes writing shorter pieces in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 reading standards for informational texts to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading informational texts to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 reading standards for literature to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading stories and literature to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find evidence in stories or information texts to support their writing and thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Provide a list of sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work.
description_plain: Your child can summarize or put information into their own words when taking notes or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall and gather relevant information from experiences and multiple print and digital sources;
description_plain: Your child remembers and gathers important information from experiences and different sources like books and websites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use multiple sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one to two pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child can type one to two pages in one sitting using a keyboard.

subject: Language Arts
description: interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child works and shares ideas with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: produce and publish writing.
description_plain: produce and publish writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance as needed, use technology, including the internet, to enhance writing.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology like the internet to improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, editing, or rewriting. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 5.)
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, writes, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that fits the story or events they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and figurative and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and descriptive words to show what happened and how it felt.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, description, and pacing to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting up the situation, introducing the narrator and characters, and organizing the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events with good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly wraps up the information or explanation they have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories, paragraphs or sections of information using grade level appropriate words, phrases, and clauses.
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas within and between paragraphs using words and phrases that make the writing clear and easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by adding facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include text features and multimedia when useful to support comprehension for the reader.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, give an overview, group related information, and use things like headings, pictures, or media to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that clearly explain a topic and share ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly wraps up their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using grade level appropriate words, phrases, and clauses.
description_plain: Your child can link their opinion and reasons using words and phrases that make their ideas clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons for their opinion and support them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic or text, state their opinion, and organize their ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces about topics or texts, giving reasons and information to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the surrounding words to check and correct how they read and understand words, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level texts in a variety of genres orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and prosody (stress, phrasing, intonation, and expression) on consecutive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level stories and texts out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression that shows understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with a clear reason and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and correctly so they can understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words. Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to accurately read unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use their knowledge of sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new and longer words correctly, both in and out of context.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 5 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: This skill is not taught in grade 5; please see earlier grades for more information.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 5 standard for this concept. Please see preceding grades for more information.
description_plain: This skill is not taught in grade 5; please see earlier grades for more information.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can choose books and texts on their own for fun, interest, or school work with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g. layout text structure, language features, knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level nonfiction texts that get harder and more detailed over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational text.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write, speak or demonstrate knowledge about the subject.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from several texts about the same topic and use it to write, talk, or show what they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain and identify how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different books or websites to quickly find answers or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can compare different accounts of the same event or topic and notice important similarities and differences in how they are told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall text structure and the presentation of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts (description, chronology, comparison, cause/effect, and problem/solution).
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts organize and present events, ideas, or information in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of general and subject-specific words and phrases in a fifth-grade text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships and/or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, concepts, or steps in procedures in historical, scientific, or technical text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, ideas, or steps in a process are connected in history, science, or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can accurately quote from a text to explain what it says and to support their own ideas or conclusions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child can pick books and stories on their own for fun, interest, or school with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend proficiently at grade level with increasing challenge in text difficulty and complexity (e.g. layout text structure, language/literary features, knowledge demands).
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level stories and literature that get harder and more detailed over time.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4-5 text complexity band independently and proficiently. By the end of the year, read and comprehend a variety of literary text.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at a fifth-grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the authors' approach to similar themes and topics within the same genre.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different authors write about similar themes and topics in the same type of story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or mood of a text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, and poem).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures and multimedia parts add to the meaning, feeling, or mood of a story, poem, or other text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the narrator's or speaker's point of view affects how the story is told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or stanzas fit together to form the whole story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language and connotative meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including metaphors and similes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast characters, settings, or events in a story or play, using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem and explain how it is supported by details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, drama, or poem and explain it using details, including how characters react to problems or how a poem’s speaker thinks about a topic; they can also summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can accurately quote from a story or poem to explain what it says and to support their own ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (for research, reflection and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, reasons, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from informational texts and/or literary fiction or nonfiction texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important details from books and articles to support their writing and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can include quotes or ideas from others in their writing without copying, and they know how to give credit properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can check if the information sources they use are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use search terms effectively.
description_plain: Use search terms effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources.
description_plain: Your child can gather useful information from many books, websites, and other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer a question, using information from different sources and coming up with new, related questions to explore further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and clearly show how their ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can plan, write, and improve their work by revising and editing to better fit their purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and the people who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.
description_plain: This standard does not apply as a separate requirement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or paragraph that matches and supports the information they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child understands how to write in a formal and neutral way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that fit well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into main groups, and use things like headings, pictures, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or give information, including telling about historical events, science experiments, or how things work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or paragraph that matches and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words transitional, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use connecting words and phrases to link ideas and show how claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with good reasons and accurate information from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can clearly state their opinion on a topic, recognize other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in social studies.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science or technical texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare what they learn from experiments, videos, or other media with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, bias, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, bias, and guesses in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table etc.).
description_plain: Your child can connect information given in words with information shown in charts, graphs, or pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why the author is explaining something, describing steps, or talking about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text is organized and how each part helps them understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6–8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what symbols, important words, and special terms mean in science or technical texts for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and precisely follow a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary that is not just their own opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of science or technical texts to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history or social studies texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand account (primary source) and a secondhand account (secondary source) about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, bias, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, bias, and thoughtful conclusions in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps etc.) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from pictures, charts, or maps with written or digital text to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can spot parts of a text that show the author's opinion or goal, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the structure of a text (e.g., sequence, comparison/contrast, cause/effect, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text is organized, such as by order of events, comparing ideas, or showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a history or social studies text, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered, etc.)
description_plain: Your child can identify important steps in a text that explains a process in history or social studies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a primary or secondary source and give a summary in their own words, not just what they already know or think.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of primary and secondary sources to support their analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use new words that are important for school subjects and know how to find out what unfamiliar words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feelings or ideas between words that have similar meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to each other to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification, etc.) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like when things are described as if they were alive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words are connected, and the slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check if their guess about a word's meaning is correct.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use books or online tools to find how to say a word, what it means, or what kind of word it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the rest of the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or explain the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child can keep the same style and tone throughout their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of sentences to make their writing or speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly; consult references as needed
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly and knows how to check spelling when unsure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child can notice when their own or others' writing or speaking doesn't follow standard English and knows how to fix it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns in writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child can find and correct mistakes when pronouns don't match in number or person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves, etc.).
description_plain: Your child understands how to use words like myself and ourselves to emphasize who is doing an action.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, and possessive).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns in the correct form, like subject, object, or possessive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts, audience, and tasks, using feedback from self and others and demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 6 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on who they are talking to and the situation, using feedback to improve and speaking formally when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or videos to presentations to help explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas clearly in order, using facts and details, while speaking with good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate (break down) a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s main points and tell which ideas have good reasons or evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand information shown in different ways, like pictures or charts, and explain how it relates to what they are learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate, mediate, and problem solve to make decisions as appropriate for productive group discussion.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others by cooperating, solving problems, and helping the group make good decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can review and show understanding of different viewpoints by reflecting on and restating what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions with detailed comments that add to the discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow discussion rules, set goals and deadlines, and take on roles when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses evidence to think about and discuss ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group discussions, listen to others, build on their ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences; independently select writing topics and formats for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and audiences, choosing their own topics and types of writing for fun and school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards for informational texts to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading informational texts to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards for literature to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading stories and literature to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from literary or informational texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds important details from books or articles to support their writing and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can quote or restate information from others without copying, and knows how to give credit to the sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility of each source.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a source of information is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from many books and websites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects using several sources and adjust their questions as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others. Demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type produce writing with a minimum of two-three pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the internet to write, share their work, and work with others, typing enough to write two or three pages in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach. Use guidance and support from peers and adults as needed. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 6.).
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, drafting, revising, and editing, with help from others when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and/or reflects the narrated experiences or event (when appropriate to the genre).
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits the story or experience they are sharing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, figurative and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and interesting words and details to describe events and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use literary and narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, rhythm, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use things like dialogue, pacing, rhythm, and description to make stories and characters more interesting and real.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing the main character or narrator, and organizing events in a way that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives and other creative texts to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories or creative pieces that show real or imagined events with clear details and a good order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that fits with the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words, including subject-related vocabulary, to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect ideas and show how they relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by adding facts, definitions, examples, quotes, and other details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings, etc.), graphics (e.g., charts, tables, etc.), and multimedia when useful to aid comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, organize their ideas clearly, and use things like headings, pictures, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that clearly explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that matches and wraps up their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can back up their opinions with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child can state their main point and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
