description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational text in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently, with guidance and support as needed at the high end of the range. a. Read and comprehend with proficiency at grade level. b. Self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks. c. Read widely to understand multiple perspectives and diverse viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand informational texts for their grade level, pick books they enjoy or need for school, and read different kinds of viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two authors' presentations of events on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two authors tell about the same event in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments in a text, telling which claims are supported by reasons and evidence and which are not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or visual formats as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can put together information from pictures, videos, and words to understand a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out an author's point of view or purpose and explain how the text shows it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a text fit together and help develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or descriptive meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain in detail how a key person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details. Provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, see how details show it, and tell what the text is about without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as logical inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the text to explain what it says clearly and to make smart guesses about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read widely to understand multiple perspectives and diverse viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child reads many different kinds of books to understand different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child picks books to read for fun, interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend with proficiency at grade level.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands books at their grade level well.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently, with guidance and support as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems written for grades 6–8, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories, etc.) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories and poems or different types of books to see how they handle similar themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing the differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a specific sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole story and helps build the main idea, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including figurative language, and explain how word choice affects tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story or play's plot develops and how characters change as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea(s) of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea in a story, see how details show it, and tell what the story is about without sharing personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as logical inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find parts of a story to explain what it says clearly and to make smart guesses about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (for research, reflection and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, reasons, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from informational texts and/or literary fiction or nonfiction texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important details from books and articles to support their writing and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can include quotes or ideas from others in their writing without copying, and they know how to give credit properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can check if the information sources they use are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use search terms effectively.
description_plain: Use search terms effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources.
description_plain: Your child can gather useful information from many books, websites, and other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer a question, using information from different sources and coming up with new, related questions to explore further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and clearly show how their ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can plan, write, and improve their work by revising and editing to better fit their purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and the people who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.
description_plain: This standard does not apply as a separate requirement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or paragraph that matches and supports the information they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child understands how to write in a formal and neutral way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that fit well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into main groups, and use things like headings, pictures, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or give information, including telling about historical events, science experiments, or how things work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or paragraph that matches and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words transitional, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use connecting words and phrases to link ideas and show how claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with good reasons and accurate information from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can clearly state their opinion on a topic, recognize other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in social studies.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science or technical texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare what they learn from experiments, videos, or other media with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, bias, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, bias, and guesses in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table etc.).
description_plain: Your child can connect information given in words with information shown in charts, graphs, or pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why the author is explaining something, describing steps, or talking about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text is organized and how each part helps them understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6–8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what symbols, important words, and special terms mean in science or technical texts for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and precisely follow a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary that is not just their own opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of science or technical texts to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history or social studies texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand account (primary source) and a secondhand account (secondary source) about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, bias, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, bias, and thoughtful conclusions in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps etc.) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from pictures, charts, or maps with written or digital text to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can spot parts of a text that show the author's opinion or goal, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the structure of a text (e.g., sequence, comparison/contrast, cause/effect, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text is organized, such as by order of events, comparing ideas, or showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a history or social studies text, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered, etc.)
description_plain: Your child can identify important steps in a text that explains a process in history or social studies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a primary or secondary source and give a summary in their own words, not just what they already know or think.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of primary and secondary sources to support their analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and accurately use grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words that are important for school and everyday life to understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings or associations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech, including allusions (e.g., literary, biblical, mythological in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains phrases that mean more than their words, like references to stories, myths, or the Bible.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check to make sure they have found the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials, both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses books or online tools to find out how to say a word, what it means, or what kind of word it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the surrounding text to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases in their reading by using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening. Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening, and chooses words that are clear and not repetitive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly; consult references as needed.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly and looks up words when unsure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie).
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly between adjectives that equally describe something, like 'a fun, exciting game.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix sentences where descriptive words or phrases are in the wrong place or unclear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence.
description_plain: Your child can put phrases and clauses correctly inside sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child will choose different types of sentences (simple, compound, complex) to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what phrases and clauses do in a sentence and how they work in specific examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts, audiences, and tasks, using self-reflection and feedback from others and demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 7 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child will change how they speak depending on who they are talking to and what the situation is, using feedback to improve and speaking formally when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other visuals in presentations to help explain their ideas and highlight important points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share ideas and important details when speaking, using good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate (break down) a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's main points and decide if their reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand main ideas and details from different types of media and explain how they help explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate, mediate, and problem solve to make decisions as appropriate for productive group discussion.
description_plain: Your child works well with others by helping solve problems and making decisions during group talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new information from others and change their own opinions when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that encourage others to explain more, and respond to others with comments that keep the discussion on track.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow discussion rules, keep track of goals and deadlines, and help define roles in group work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes prepared for discussions by reading or researching, and uses evidence from their preparation to share and reflect on ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions on 7th grade topics, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences; independently select writing topics and formats for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and audiences, choosing topics and styles on their own for fun and school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards for informational texts to writing
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading informational texts to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards for literature to writing
description_plain: Your child uses what they learn from reading stories and literature to help with their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from literary or informational texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds important details from stories or information texts to support their writing and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child follows standard rules when giving credit to sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child includes quotes or restates others' ideas in their writing without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child checks if the sources they use are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use search terms effectively.
description_plain: Use search terms effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources.
description_plain: Your child gathers useful information from books, websites, and other places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer a question, using several sources and coming up with new, related questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to produce writing with stamina in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child uses technology, like the Internet, to write, publish, and share their work, and to work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. Use guidance and support from peers and adults as needed. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 7.)
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, and editing, with help from others when needed, focusing on their purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and/or reflects on the narrated experiences or events (when appropriate to genre).
description_plain: Your child writes endings that connect to and reflect on the story or events they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and figurative and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and descriptive words, including sensory details, to show actions and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative and literary techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, rhythm, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue and pacing to develop events and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that introduce characters and events in a clear and logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives and other creative texts to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories or creative pieces that show real or imagined events with clear details and order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes an ending that fits and supports the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transitions to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child explains their topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings, etc.), graphics (e.g., charts, tables, etc.), and multimedia when useful to aid comprehension.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces the topic, organizes information in a logical way, and uses things like headings, graphics, or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that explain a topic clearly by choosing, organizing, and analyzing the right information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes an ending that fits and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitional words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words and phrases to connect their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their argument with logical reasons and evidence from reliable sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main argument, mention other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their ideas with clear reasons and evidence that makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read widely to understand multiple perspectives and diverse viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child reads many different texts to understand different opinions and viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child chooses books and texts to read for fun, interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend with proficiency at grade level.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand texts at a seventh-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction and informational text in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently, with guidance and support as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child will read and understand nonfiction and informational texts for grades 6–8 on their own, asking for help only with the hardest parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different authors write about the same topic, noticing what evidence they use and how they interpret facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess whether the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can decide if the evidence in a text is enough and relevant to support the author's points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess whether the reasoning is sound.
description_plain: Your child can tell if the author's reasoning makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the main arguments and specific claims in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version, noticing how each one shows the subject differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her viewpoint from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's opinion or purpose and explain how it is different from others' views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can examine how an author organizes a text and how each section helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze how a specific word choice affects meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and see how word choices affect meaning and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas affect each other in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear and unbiased summary of what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze their development over the course of the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the main ideas develop throughout the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from a text to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read widely to understand multiple perspectives and diverse viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child reads many different kinds of books to understand different opinions and viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child chooses books to read for fun, interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend with proficiency at grade level.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories and literature at a seventh-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently, with guidance and support as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems written for grades 6-8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period to understand how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare how a story or novel shows a time, place, or character with how it is described in real history, to see how fiction changes or uses history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, movie, stage, or multimedia version, noticing how each one uses different techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the overall form or structure of a text (e.g., drama, poetry, narrative, short story) contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the way a story, poem, or play is organized helps show its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including how figurative and connotative meanings affect tone; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including special meanings and sounds like rhymes or alliteration, and how these affect the feeling or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play, like setting and characters, work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear and unbiased summary of a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze its development over the course of the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the theme or main idea develops throughout a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea(s) of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme or main ideas in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use several pieces of evidence from a story or play to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (for research, reflection and revision) and shorter time frames for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, reasons, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from informational texts and/or literary fiction or nonfiction texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important details from books and articles to support their writing and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can include quotes or ideas from others in their writing without copying, and they know how to give credit properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can check if the information sources they use are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use search terms effectively.
description_plain: Use search terms effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources.
description_plain: Your child can gather useful information from many books, websites, and other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer a question, using information from different sources and coming up with new, related questions to explore further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and clearly show how their ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can plan, write, and improve their work by revising and editing to better fit their purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and the people who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable as a separate requirement.
description_plain: This standard does not apply as a separate requirement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or paragraph that matches and supports the information they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child understands how to write in a formal and neutral way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that fit well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into main groups, and use things like headings, pictures, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or give information, including telling about historical events, science experiments, or how things work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or paragraph that matches and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words transitional, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use connecting words and phrases to link ideas and show how claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with good reasons and accurate information from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can clearly state their opinion on a topic, recognize other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in social studies.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science or technical texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare what they learn from experiments, videos, or other media with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, bias, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, bias, and guesses in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table etc.).
description_plain: Your child can connect information given in words with information shown in charts, graphs, or pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why the author is explaining something, describing steps, or talking about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text is organized and how each part helps them understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6–8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what symbols, important words, and special terms mean in science or technical texts for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and precisely follow a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary that is not just their own opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of science or technical texts to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history or social studies texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand account (primary source) and a secondhand account (secondary source) about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, bias, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, bias, and thoughtful conclusions in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps etc.) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from pictures, charts, or maps with written or digital text to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can spot parts of a text that show the author's opinion or goal, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the structure of a text (e.g., sequence, comparison/contrast, cause/effect, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text is organized, such as by order of events, comparing ideas, or showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a history or social studies text, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered, etc.)
description_plain: Your child can identify important steps in a text that explains a process in history or social studies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a primary or secondary source and give a summary in their own words, not just what they already know or think.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of primary and secondary sources to support their analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and accurately use grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words correctly and notices important words to understand or express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings or associations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to each other to better understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g. verbal irony, puns) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like irony and puns in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can double-check that they have figured out the correct meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses books or online tools to find how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, and secede).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different strategies to understand words they don’t know or words with more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (e.g. expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).
description_plain: Your child can use verbs that show uncertainty or talk about things that aren't true to make their writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice to achieve particular effects (e.g., emphasizing the actor or the action).
description_plain: Your child can use active and passive verbs to focus on who is doing something or on the action itself.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use an ellipsis (...) to show that something has been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation like commas, ellipses, and dashes to show pauses or breaks in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when the verb voice or mood changes inappropriately in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in different moods (like statements, commands, questions, possibilities, or wishes) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
