description_plain: Your child can use verbs in both active and passive voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbal's (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands how special verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives work in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, parts of the word, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to make meaning clear when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using self-reflection and feedback from others and demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 8 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using feedback and speaking clearly and correctly when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other visuals in presentations to make information clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share their ideas and findings using strong reasons and details, while speaking loudly enough and making eye contact.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate (break down) a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can break down what a speaker is saying, check if their reasons and evidence make sense, and notice when something doesn't relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas and details in different types of media and think about why it was made, like for social or political reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cooperate, mediate, and problem solve to make decisions as appropriate for productive group discussion.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others by cooperating, solving problems, and helping make good group decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new ideas from others and changes or explains their own opinions when the evidence supports it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that connect what different people are saying and answer others with good evidence or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow group discussion rules, keep track of goals and deadlines, and help decide who does what in a group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or researched the topic, and uses what they learned to talk about the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to share information and help others understand presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and understand the purpose and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions, reasons, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different types of media like videos, charts, and speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and take part in conversations and group work, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames in for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences; independently select writing topics and formats for personal enjoyment, interest, and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different reasons and can choose their own topics and styles for fun or school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to informational and argumentative texts
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to understand and write about facts and arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literature and literary nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to understand and write about stories and true-life stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds evidence in stories or informational texts to support their thoughts, reflections, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can include information from others by quoting or rewording it correctly and without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can check if the information sources they use are trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use search terms effectively
description_plain: Use search terms effectively

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from many books, websites, and other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new, related questions to explore the topic further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share, and work together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, drafting, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1–3 up to and including grade 8.)
description_plain: With some help, your child plans, writes, revises, and edits their writing to make it clearer and better for their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events (when appropriate to genre).
description_plain: Your child can write endings that make sense and reflect on the story or events they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory and figurative language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and detailed words, including sensory and creative language, to show what is happening and how it feels.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another, and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to show the order of events, changes in time or place, and how events are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative and literary techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, rhythm, description, and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling tools like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop characters and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, and tells events in a clear, natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives and other creative texts to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories or creative pieces that show real or imagined events with good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits and supports the information or explanation they have given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style appropriate for the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by using relevant facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into categories, and use helpful features like headings, graphics, or multimedia.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic clearly by choosing, organizing, and analyzing relevant information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style that fits the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitional words, phrases, and clauses to crate cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their arguments clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their argument with logical reasons and evidence from reliable sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main argument, explain how it is different from other viewpoints, and organize reasons and evidence logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their ideas with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, propose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and logically with the right style for the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, ad well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write essays that explain their ideas clearly and use good reasons and facts to support their opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read widely to understand multiple perspectives and diverse viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child reads many different texts to understand different opinions and points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child picks books and texts to read for fun, interest, or school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend with proficiency at grade level.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level nonfiction texts well.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction and informational text in the grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child will read and understand nonfiction texts for grades 6–8 on their own by the end of the year.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or identify where the texts disagree on matters of interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts on the same topic, find where they disagree, and explain the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can tell when information in a text does not relate to the main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess whether the evidence is relevant and sufficient.
description_plain: Your child can decide if the evidence in a text is important and enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess whether the reasoning is sound.
description_plain: Your child can judge if the reasons given in a text make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate (break down) and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text;
description_plain: Your child can break down and evaluate the main argument and specific claims in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child can think about the good and bad points of using different ways like print, video, or digital to share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and explain how the author deals with opposing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can closely examine a paragraph to see how each sentence helps explain or develop a main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze how specific word choices, including analogies or allusions to other texts on affect meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and feeling of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text connects or compares people, ideas, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear and unbiased summary of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the central idea develops throughout the text and connects to supporting details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text
description_plain: Your child understands how to find the main idea of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the strongest evidence from a text to explain what it says and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read widely to understand multiple perspectives and pluralistic viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child reads many different texts to understand various viewpoints and opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Self-select texts for personal enjoyment, interest and academic tasks.
description_plain: Your child chooses books to read for fun, interest, or schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend with proficiency at grade level.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand grade-level literature well.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of grades 6–8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how modern stories use ideas, events, or characters from old myths, traditional tales, or religious stories, and describe what makes them new or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature
description_plain: Not applicable to literature

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a filmed or live production of a story, drama, or poem stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or play version of a story, drama, or poem to the original text and explain the director’s or actors’ choices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audience or reader (e.g., created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different points of view in stories create feelings like suspense or humor.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more stories are organized and explain how those differences affect the meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, figurative and connotative meanings; analyze how specific word choices, including sound devices, analogies or allusions to other texts, affect meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including figurative language, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character, or provoke a decision.
description_plain: Your child can explain how specific dialogue or events in a story move the plot forward, show character traits, or cause decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear and unbiased summary of a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the theme develops through the characters, setting, and plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea(s) of a text and
description_plain: Your child understands how to find the theme or main idea of a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the best evidence from stories to explain what they say and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem in different artistic mediums, evaluating how each version interprets the source text.
description_plain: Your child can compare different versions of a story, play, or poem in various art forms and explain how each one shows the original in a unique way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how point of view and/or author purpose requires distinguishing what is directly stated in text and what is implied.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose helps decide what is clearly said and what is suggested in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how point of view and/or author purpose requires distinguishing what is directly stated in text and what is implied.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose helps decide what is clearly said and what is suggested in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's choices about where to start or end a story and how to finish it affect the story’s meaning and feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's choices about where to start or end a story and how to finish it affect the story’s meaning and feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words have special or multiple meanings, and how these choices affect the story’s mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words have special or multiple meanings, and how these choices affect the story’s mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters are introduced and developed).
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author develops parts of a story, like the setting, order of events, and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters are introduced and developed).
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author develops parts of a story, like the setting, order of events, and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text to support thematic analysis.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas or themes in a story and explain how they grow and connect throughout the text, using evidence from the story to support their explanation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text to support thematic analysis.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas or themes in a story and explain how they grow and connect throughout the text, using evidence from the story to support their explanation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Accurately cite strong, relevant, and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.
description_plain: Your child can find and use strong examples from the text to explain what it says clearly and what it suggests, including parts that are unclear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Accurately cite strong, relevant, and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.
description_plain: Your child can find and use strong examples from the text to explain what it says clearly and what it suggests, including parts that are unclear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main arguments and evidence in a text to see if they make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author's point of view or purpose changes what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs fit together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or tricky meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into ten ones and some further ones, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each composition or decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 18 = 10 + 8); understand that these numbers are composed of ten ones and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together numbers from 11 to 19 using groups of ten and extra ones, and show this with drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Compose simple shapes to form larger shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put simple shapes together to make bigger shapes.

subject: Math
description: Model shapes in the world by building shapes from components (e.g., sticks and clay balls) and drawing shapes.
description_plain: Your child can build shapes using parts like sticks and clay and draw shapes they see around them.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and compare two- and three-dimensional shapes, in different sizes and orientations, using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts (e.g., number of sides and vertices/"corners") and other attributes (e.g., having sides of equal length).
description_plain: Your child can look at and compare flat and solid shapes of different sizes and positions, and talk about how they are alike or different, including parts like sides and corners.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional (lying in a plane, "flat") or three-dimensional ("solid").
description_plain: Your child can tell if a shape is flat (2D) or solid (3D).

subject: Math
description: Correctly name shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size.
description_plain: Your child can name shapes correctly no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes, and describe the relative positions of these objects using terms such as above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.
description_plain: Your child can use shape names to describe objects and use words like above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to to describe where things are.

subject: Math
description: Identify a penny and understand that the value is one. Count pennies within 20.
description_plain: Your child can recognize a penny, knows it’s worth one cent, and can count up to 20 pennies.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects into given categories; count the number of objects in each category and sort the categories by count. Limit category counts to be less than or equal to 10.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups, count how many are in each group, and arrange the groups by size, with counts up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has "more of"/"less of" the attribute, and describe the difference.
description_plain: Your child can compare two objects to see which one has more or less of something and explain the difference.

subject: Math
description: Describe measurable attributes of a single object or objects, such as length, weight, or size.
description_plain: Your child can describe things by their size, length, or weight.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 5.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 5.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record the answer with a drawing or equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the number that adds to another number to make 10, using objects or drawings, and show the answer with a picture or equation.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 5 = 2 + 3 and 5 = 4 + 1).
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers up to 10 in different ways using objects or drawings and write each way as a picture or equation.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 10, eg., by using objects or drawings to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 10 using objects or drawings to help.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems (within 10), involving result unknown problems, put together/take apart total unknown, and put together/take apart addend unknown, e.g., using objects or drawings to represent the problem. (see appendix for K-2 Common Addition and Subtraction Situations)
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction word problems up to 10 by using objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction word problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds (e.g., claps), acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations. (Drawings need not show details, but should show the mathematics in the problem.)
description_plain: Your child can show addition and subtraction using things like objects, fingers, drawings, sounds, acting, or words.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers from 1 to 10 when they are written as numerals.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group. Include groups with up to ten objects.
description_plain: Your child can tell if one group of up to 10 objects has more, less, or the same number as another group.

subject: Math
description: Given a number(s) from 1–20, count out that many objects.
description_plain: Your child can count out the right number of objects when given a number from 1 to 20.

subject: Math
description: When counting, answer questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle, or and as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 objects arranged in lines, rectangles, or circles, and up to 10 objects scattered around.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?"
description_plain: Count to answer "how many?"

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.
description_plain: Your child understands that when you say numbers in order, each number is one more than the one before.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. (cardinality) The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.
description_plain: Your child understands that the last number they say when counting tells how many objects there are, no matter how the objects are arranged.

subject: Math
description: When counting objects, say the number names in the standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object. (one-to-one correspondence)
description_plain: Your child can count objects by saying number names in order, matching one number to each object and one object to each number.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers relate to amounts and connects counting to knowing how many things there are.

subject: Math
description: Write numbers from 0 to 20. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0-20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects).
description_plain: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20 and use these numbers to show how many objects there are.

subject: Math
description: Count forward beginning from any given number within 100 (instead of having to begin at 1). Count backwards beginning from any given number within 20.
description_plain: Your child can count forward starting from any number up to 100 and count backward starting from any number up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 (positive or zero differences), using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can subtract multiples of 10 from other multiples of 10 (like 30 - 20) using drawings or objects and explain how they did it.

subject: Math
description: Given a two-digit number, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than the number, without having to count; explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can quickly find 10 more or 10 less than any two-digit number and explain how they know.

subject: Math
description: Understand that in adding two-digit numbers (sums within 100) add tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.
description_plain: Your child understands how to add two-digit numbers by adding tens to tens and ones to ones, and sometimes making a new ten.

subject: Math
description: Add within 100, including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number, and adding a two-digit number and a multiple of 10, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add numbers up to 100, including adding two-digit numbers with one-digit numbers or multiples of ten, using drawings or objects and explaining their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 100.
description_plain: Add and subtract within 100.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols <, =, and >.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers by looking at their tens and ones and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine tens (and 0 ones).
description_plain: Your child knows that numbers like 10, 20, 30, and so on mean one, two, three, etc., groups of ten.

subject: Math
description: The numbers from 11 to 19 are composed of a ten and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers from 11 to 19 are made of one ten and some ones.

subject: Math
description: 10 can be thought of as a bundle of ten ones — called a "ten."
description_plain: Your child understands that 10 is a group of ten ones called a 'ten.'

subject: Math
description: Understand that the two digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones. Understand the following as special cases:
description_plain: Your child understands that two-digit numbers have tens and ones, and knows special cases about these numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent a number of objects with a written numeral.
description_plain: Your child can write numbers to show how many objects there are.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numerals.
description_plain: Read and write numerals.

subject: Math
description: Count on from any given number.
description_plain: Your child can count forward starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: In the range of 0 - 120
description_plain: In the range of 0 - 120

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, fourths, and quarters, and use the phrases half of, fourth of, and quarter of. Describe the whole as two of, or four of the shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts, use words like halves and quarters to describe them, and understand that more parts mean smaller pieces.

subject: Math
description: Compose and Identify regular and irregular two-dimensional shapes (rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles, and quarter-circles) and compose three-dimensional shapes (cubes, spheres, right rectangular prisms, right circular cones, and right circular cylinders) to create a composite shape, and compose new shapes from the composite shape. (Students do not need to master formal names such as "right rectangular prism.")
description_plain: Your child can recognize and make flat shapes like squares and triangles, and solid shapes like cubes and spheres, and put them together to make new shapes.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between defining attributes (e.g., triangles are closed and three-sided) versus non-defining attributes (e.g., color, orientation, overall size); build and draw shapes to possess defining attributes.
description_plain: Your child understands the important features that define shapes, like a triangle having three sides, and can build and draw shapes showing those features.

subject: Math
description: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories; ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and how many more or less are in one category than in another.
description_plain: Your child can collect and show information in up to three groups, and ask and answer questions about how many items are in each group and how they compare.

subject: Math
description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units, by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end; understand that the length measurement of an object is the number of same-size length units that span it with no gaps or overlaps.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up smaller objects of the same size without gaps or overlaps and count how many it takes.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can put three objects in order from shortest to longest and compare two objects by using a third object as a reference.

subject: Math
description: Identify nickels and understand that five pennies can be thought of as a nickel. Identify dimes and understand ten pennies can be thought of as a dime. Count the value of a set of coins comprised of pennies, nickels, and dimes.
description_plain: Your child can identify nickels and dimes, understand that five pennies equal a nickel and ten pennies equal a dime, and count the total value of pennies, nickels, and dimes.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the hour and half-hour using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating to three whole numbers. For example, determine the unknown number that makes the equation true in each of the equations 8 +? = 11, 5 = ? – 3, 6 + 6 = ? .
description_plain: Your child can find the missing number in addition or subtraction problems, like figuring out what number makes 8 + ? = 11 true.

subject: Math
description: Understand the meaning of the equal sign, and determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false. For example, which of the following equations are true and which are false? 6 = 6, 7 = 8 – 1, 5 + 2 = 2 + 5, 4 + 1 = 5 + 2.
description_plain: Your child understands what the equal sign means and can tell if addition or subtraction equations are true or false.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10. Use strategies such as counting on; making ten (e.g., 8 + 6 = 8 + 2 + 4 = 10 + 4 = 14); decomposing a number leading to a ten (e.g., 13 – 4 = 13 – 3 – 1 = 10 – 1 = 9); using the relationship between addition and subtraction (e.g., knowing that 8 + 4 = 12, one knows 12 – 8 = 4); and creating equivalent but easier or known sums (e.g., adding 6 + 7 by creating the known equivalent 6 + 6 + 1 = 12 + 1 = 13).
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20 quickly, using helpful strategies like counting on or making ten.

subject: Math
description: Understand counting on as addition and counting back as subtraction e.g. 5, (6,7,8) means 5 + 3 and 5, (4,3,2) means 5-3.
description_plain: Your child understands that adding means counting up and subtracting means counting back from a number.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction as an unknown-addend problem. For example, subtract 10 – 8 by finding the number that makes 10 when added to 8.
description_plain: Your child can solve subtraction problems by thinking about what number added to the smaller number equals the bigger number.

subject: Math
description: Apply commutative, associative, and additive identity properties of operations as strategies to add. (Students need not use formal terms for these properties.) Examples: If 8 + 3 = 11 is known, then 3 + 8 = 11 is also known. (Commutative property of addition.) To add 2 + 6 + 4, the second two numbers can be added to make a ten, so 2 + 6 + 4 = 2 + 10 = 12. (Associative property of addition.) 8 + 0 = 8 (Additive Identity property)
description_plain: Your child can use simple tricks to add numbers, like switching the order of numbers or grouping some numbers first to make adding easier.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add three whole numbers with a total of 20 or less.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems with addition and subtraction up to 20, including problems where the missing number can be anywhere.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Explain why addition and subtraction strategies work, using place value and the properties of operations. (Explanations may be supported by words, drawings or objects.)
description_plain: Your child can explain why addition and subtraction methods work by using place value and number properties, sometimes with drawings or objects.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number 100–900, and mentally subtract 10 or 100 from a given number 100–900.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add or subtract 10 or 100 to numbers between 100 and 900 in their head.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 1000, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method. Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1000 using drawings or objects and understands how to add or subtract hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Add up to four two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can add up to four two-digit numbers using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 100 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers within 100 using place value and number properties.

subject: Math
description: Compare, two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ones digits, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers by looking at their hundreds, tens, and ones places and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers to 1000 using base-ten numerals (standard form), number names (word form), and expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 1000 in regular number form, word form, and expanded form (showing the value of each digit).

subject: Math
description: Count within 1000; skip-count by 5s, 10s, and 100s, starting from any number in its skip counting sequence.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 1000 and skip count by 5s, 10s, and 100s starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine hundreds (and 0 tens and 0 ones).
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers like 100, 200, 300, up to 900 mean one to nine hundreds with zero tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: 100 can be thought of as a bundle of ten tens — called a "hundred."
description_plain: Your child understands that 100 is a group of ten tens called a 'hundred.'

subject: Math
description: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones; e.g., 706 equals 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones. Understand the following as special cases:
description_plain: Your child understands that three-digit numbers have hundreds, tens, and ones, like 706 means 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc., and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal parts, use words like halves and thirds to describe them, and understand that equal parts can look different.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into rows and columns of equal squares and count how many squares there are.

subject: Math
description: Recognize, identify, and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces; to include triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes. (Sizes are compared directly or visually, not compared by measuring.)
description_plain: Your child can recognize, name, and draw shapes by looking at their features like the number of sides or faces, including triangles, squares, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, …, and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can show whole numbers on a number line starting at zero and use the number line to add and subtract numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as drawings of rulers) and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve problems about lengths measured in the same units.

subject: Math
description: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure how much longer one object is than another using standard units like inches or centimeters.

subject: Math
description: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.
description_plain: Your child can guess lengths using units like inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.
description_plain: Your child can measure the same object twice using different units and explain how the measurements relate to the size of the units.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.
description_plain: Your child can measure the length of an object using tools like rulers, yardsticks, or measuring tapes.

subject: Math
description: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph.
description_plain: Your child can draw picture and bar graphs with up to four groups and use them to solve simple problems about adding, taking away, or comparing amounts.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object. Show the measurements by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.
description_plain: Your child can measure several objects and show the measurements on a line plot using whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using dollar bills and coins like quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, and use $ and ¢ symbols correctly.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and know the value of coins up to one dollar.
description_plain: Your child knows the value of coins up to one dollar.

subject: Math
description: Identify and count coins and bills and apply that understanding to solve word problems.
description_plain: Your child can identify and count coins and bills and use that knowledge to solve money problems.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes using clocks and know if it is a.m. or p.m.

subject: Math
description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rows and columns (up to 5 rows and 5 columns) and write an equation showing equal groups added together.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members, e.g., by pairing objects or counting them by 2s; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects has an odd or even number by pairing them or counting by twos, and can write an equation showing even numbers as two equal parts.

subject: Math
description: By end of Grade 2, know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers.
description_plain: By the end of second grade, your child can quickly recall all addition sums of two one-digit numbers from memory.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies. (See standard 1.OA.6 for a list of mental strategies.)
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 20 easily using mental math strategies.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Add and subtract within 20.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step word problems with addition and subtraction within 100 by using drawings or simple equations with a missing number.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols < , =, or >.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions that have the same top number or the same bottom number by thinking about their size, knowing the fractions must be parts of the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions, and recognize fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write whole numbers as fractions and recognize when fractions are equal to whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate simple equivalent fractions, e.g., ½ = 2/4, 4/6 = 2/3. Explain why the fractions are equivalent, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain simple equivalent fractions using pictures or models.

subject: Math
description: Understand two fractions as equivalent (equal) if they are the same size, or the same point on a number line.
description_plain: Your child understands that two fractions are equal if they represent the same amount or point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions in special cases, and compare fractions by reasoning about their size. Note - Grade 3 expectations in this domain are limited to fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.
description_plain: Your child can explain when fractions are equal and compare fractions by thinking about their size.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction a/b on a number line diagram by marking off a lengths 1/b from 0. Recognize that the resulting interval has size a/b and that its endpoint locates the number a/b on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show a fraction a/b on a number line by marking off a parts of size 1/b from zero.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction 1/b on a number line diagram by defining the interval from 0 to 1 as the whole and partitioning it into b equal parts. Recognize that each part has size 1/b and that the endpoint of the part based at 0 locates the number 1/b on the number line.
